title: France to fly two astronauts on Vast missions
summary: Vast has partnered with the French government to send two French astronauts on its missions, including the first flight to its Haven-1 space station. This marks a significant step for France's involvement in commercial space station operations.

title: Northrop Grumman partners with Apex on space-based interceptors for Golden Dome
summary: Northrop Grumman has partnered with Apex, a Los Angeles-based startup, to develop space-based interceptors for the Golden Dome program. Apex specializes in producing standardized, cost-effective satellite buses.

title: Spaceport facility bonds are now law – and they fundamentally change space infrastructure finance
summary: Spaceport facility bonds have been enacted into law, significantly altering the financing landscape for space infrastructure development. This marks a major shift in how spaceport projects will be funded moving forward.

title: China conducts surprise launch of Long March 12B, delivers Qianfan satellites on debut flight
summary: China successfully launched its reusable Long March 12B rocket on its debut flight, delivering operational Qianfan satellites to orbit without prior warning.

title: New Glenn failure worsens constrained launch market
summary: The explosion of Blue Origin's New Glenn rocket has disrupted the already constrained launch market, potentially removing the vehicle from service for a year or more.

title: China launches test direct-to-device satellites for multiple projects
summary: China launched four test satellites for direct-to-device communications using a hypergolic rocket, marking a significant step in its satellite internet projects. This follows a busy month of orbital deployments by the country.

title: FAA documents outline SpaceX plans for Starfall reentry vehicles
summary: FAA documents reveal SpaceX's plans to develop and test Starfall reentry vehicles, which could support in-space manufacturing projects. The project aims to advance reusable spacecraft technology for future missions.

title: Blue Origin gets national security launch task order hours before New Glenn explosion
summary: Blue Origin received a national security launch task order from the U.S. Space Force and National Reconnaissance Office just hours before the explosion of its New Glenn rocket. The agencies reaffirmed their commitment to Blue Origin as a partner despite the incident.

title: Space Force awards SpaceX $4.16 billion to build satellite network for airborne target tracking
summary: The U.S. Space Force awarded SpaceX a $4.16 billion contract to develop a low-orbit satellite network for tracking airborne threats like aircraft and cruise missiles. The constellation will enhance real-time surveillance capabilities for military operations.

title: Space Force’s commercial gatekeeper offers a playbook for startups seeking defense business
summary: Space Force’s commercial gatekeeper outlines key strategies for startups to engage with defense contracts, emphasizing transparency, patience, and a clear military value proposition. Col. Tim Trimailo highlights that these factors are as crucial as technical innovation for success.

title: How do you study an invisible exoplanet? Astronomers discover planetary 'fingerprints' in the rings around stars
summary: Astronomers have discovered a method to study invisible exoplanets by analyzing 'fingerprints' in the rings around stars. This technique allows them to infer the presence and mass of unseen planets through the patterns they create in these rings.

title: Rocket goes boom, satellite cameras zoom: Explosive Blue Origin damage is visible from space
summary: Satellites captured images of the damage caused by the explosion of Blue Origin's New Glenn rocket at LC-36 launchpad. The extent of the destruction is visible from orbit.

title: NASA's X-59 jet is ready to break the sound barrier for the 1st time this month
summary: NASA's X-59 jet is preparing for its first supersonic flight this month, aiming to break the sound barrier. The aircraft represents a significant milestone in supersonic travel technology.

title: It had the power! The weird origins of He-Man, Skeletor, and the 'Masters of the Universe'
summary: The article discusses the origins of Mattel's 'Masters of the Universe' toy line, which was created as a response to the success of 'Star Wars'. The franchise featured iconic characters like He-Man and Skeletor.

title: The 2026 hurricane season has begun. Will this year be calmer?
summary: The 2026 Atlantic hurricane season has started. NOAA forecasts a below-normal number of named storms this year.

title: Manhattanhenge is back! | Space photo of the day for June 1, 2026
summary: Manhattanhenge, a celestial event where the sun aligns with Manhattan's street grid, will occur again in 2026 with opportunities to view it in June and July.

title: 3 bright planets light up June's night sky — Here's where and when to look
summary: June's night sky will feature three bright planets—Venus, Jupiter, and Mercury—offering a spectacular viewing opportunity. Stargazers can observe these planets with the right timing and location.

title: Astronomers discover a 'lost world' of black hole mergers: 'It's the astronomical equivalent of uncovering an ancient civilization'
summary: Astronomers have discovered a hidden population of black hole mergers, described as a 'lost world' of cosmic events. This finding could significantly alter our understanding of how black holes collide.

title: NASA Awards Modification Contract for Reduced Gravity Test Aircraft
summary: NASA awarded a modification contract to Denmar Technical Services for aircraft modifications and maintenance for reduced gravity testing. The contract covers services for NASA's Armstrong Flight Research Center and Johnson Space Center.

title: NASA Invites Media to See Roman Space Telescope Arrive at Kennedy
summary: NASA has opened registration for media to cover the arrival of the Nancy Grace Roman Space Telescope at Kennedy Space Center in Florida. The telescope will be transported via NASA's Pegasus barge from Goddard Space Flight Center.

title: NASA to Conduct Low-Altitude Flights Near Houston
summary: NASA will conduct low-altitude research flights near Houston from June 3 to June 13 as part of the Student Airborne Research Program (SARP). The mission involves five aircraft flying remote sensing payloads in parallel patterns to gather data.

title: What’s Up: June 2026 Skywatching Tips from NASA
summary: NASA shares skywatching tips for June 2026, including Venus-Jupiter conjunction, lunar occultation, and summer solstice highlights. Deep-sky objects become more visible during the month.

title: Pretty in Pink
summary: NASA's Chandra X-ray Observatory and James Webb Space Telescope captured a stunning image of the star cluster Westerlund 2, showcasing stars aged between one and three million years. The composite image combines Chandra's pink X-ray data with Webb's infrared data in multiple colors.

title: Space Out This Summer with Variety of NASA STEM Activities
summary: NASA is offering a variety of STEM activities for the summer, encouraging creativity, outdoor exploration, and future aspirations. The initiative aims to engage the public in space-related learning and discovery.

title: Gravity Waves From Super Typhoon Sinlaku
summary: Satellites observed gravity waves from Super Typhoon Sinlaku, revealing upper-atmosphere phenomena generated by the intensifying tropical cyclone. This highlights the role of space-based observations in studying extreme weather events.

title: NASA Awards Contract for Johnson Space Center Infrastructure
summary: NASA awarded a contract to seven companies for construction and infrastructure improvements at Johnson Space Center in Houston, with a potential value of up to $300 million. The upgrades include mission-support facilities, utilities, and equipment, with funding obligations required by September 30, 2026.

title: NASA Hosts SpaceX Crew-11 Astronauts for Public Event at Headquarters
summary: NASA is hosting a public event featuring three astronauts from the SpaceX Crew-11 mission at its headquarters in Washington, D.C. The event will take place on June 1 at the Webb Auditorium in the Mary W. Jackson building.

title: NASA’s Roman Space Telescope Primary Mirror Gets Last Look
summary: NASA engineers completed the final inspection of the Nancy Grace Roman Space Telescope's primary mirror. The 7.9-foot mirror will help the telescope capture wide-field images of the universe.

title: Launch Preview: SpaceX and Chinese rockets to launch internet satellites to LEO
summary: SpaceX and Chinese rockets are set to launch internet satellites to Low Earth Orbit (LEO) this week. Five missions are planned, with SpaceX and Chinese entities leading the launches.

title: Ancient black hole sheds new light on Webb’s Little Red Dots
summary: An international team used the James Webb Space Telescope to study an ancient black hole, providing new insights into Webb's 'Little Red Dots'. This discovery highlights the telescope's ability to uncover early cosmic phenomena.

title: Blue Origin’s New Glenn explodes during Static Fire test at LC-36
summary: Blue Origin's New Glenn rocket experienced a significant anomaly during a static fire test at Launch Complex 36, resulting in an explosion. The incident marks a major setback for the company's orbital launch ambitions.

title: Rovers and Drones laying the foundations for NASA Moon Base
summary: NASA has outlined initial missions for its Moon Base initiative, involving rovers and drones to lay groundwork. The initiative rebrands existing Commercial Lunar Payload Services (CLPS) missions.

title: Following Starship V3 debut, SpaceX prepares for follow up
summary: SpaceX successfully conducted the first flight of Starship Version 3 this week. The company is now preparing for a follow-up mission.

title: Launch Preview: Starlink and Amazon Leo missions fill manifest
summary: Six orbital launches are scheduled this week, with four Falcon 9 Starlink missions and Amazon's Leo project among the highlights. The launches are part of a busy manifest for space industry activities.

title: Vehicle arrivals, science experiments, and maintenance keep Expedition 74 busy through April
summary: Expedition 74 crew aboard the International Space Station has been occupied with vehicle arrivals, science experiments, and maintenance tasks throughout April. The activities highlight ongoing operations and research in low Earth orbit.

title: Shenzhou 23 launches new crew to Tiangong space station
summary: Shenzhou 23 successfully launched a new crew to China's Tiangong space station, which has been permanently occupied since June 5, 2022.

title: Starship Flight 12: Block 3 Impresses on First Flight
summary: Starship Flight 12, featuring Block 3, successfully launched on May 22, 2026, at 5:30:21 pm CDT from SpaceX's facilities. The mission demonstrated significant improvements and impressed during its first flight.

title: Launch Preview: Falcon 9, Electron, and Vega C launch missions alongside Starship Flight 12
summary: Six space launches are scheduled globally in the next week, including SpaceX's 12th Starship flight, a Falcon 9 mission, an Electron launch, and a Vega C mission.

title: TEC Business to Business website
summary: The TEC Business to Business website reported on a space industry news item, but the specific content of the news was not provided in the input.

title: ESA Delivers: 50 years booklet
summary: The European Space Agency (ESA) has released a booklet highlighting 50 significant achievements over the past 50 years. This publication celebrates the agency's milestones and contributions to space exploration.

title: ESA’s Prodex programme brings scientific research to space
summary: ESA's Prodex programme has enabled the successful launch of the 4DSpace-Daedalus mission in Norway. The programme supports research institutes in participating in European space science activities and missions.

title: Stay space chemical compliant in the EU
summary: The European Space Agency (ESA) will host a free REACH workshop on chemical compliance in the space sector on 2 June at its technical centre in the Netherlands, with an online attendance option.

title: The young minds space-proofing ESA’s missions
summary: Young researchers are testing and validating materials for ESA's missions to ensure they can withstand the harsh space environment. The Materials, Environments and Contamination Control Section is driving innovation with fresh perspectives.

title: Extended Reality at ESA opens new pathways for space exploration
summary: The European Space Agency (ESA) is leveraging Extended Reality (XR) for training, operations, simulations, and public engagement in space exploration. This initiative aims to enhance both technical and educational aspects of space missions.

title: The great parachute bake-out
summary: A space industry event involving parachute testing is described as a 'great parachute bake-out'. The term suggests a rigorous testing process for parachutes used in space missions.

title: Baking a parachute for Mars
summary: ESA is sterilizing a 35-meter parachute for the ExoMars Rosalind Franklin rover mission to ensure it is 10,000 times cleaner than a smartphone, preventing microbial contamination of Mars. The parachute, made of nylon and Kevlar, will slow the rover during its 2028 landing to search for signs of life.

title: A new grip on space: electrostatic capture technology
summary: A new electrostatic capture technology is being developed for space applications, potentially revolutionizing satellite servicing and debris removal. This innovation could enhance mission flexibility and reduce costs in orbital operations.

title: Bellatrix Aerospace Tapped to Build Korean VLEO Demo Sat
summary: Bellatrix Aerospace is collaborating with Korean optical payload manufacturer TelePIX to build a Very Low Earth Orbit (VLEO) demonstration satellite, with a planned launch no earlier than 2028.

title: Opinion: Commercial Space Is Generating Signals Decision-Makers Cannot Hear
summary: Commercial space activities are producing vast amounts of operational data, but decision-makers struggle to process these signals effectively. The existing mechanisms for filtering and prioritizing these signals are inadequate.

title: Nuggets in the SpaceX S-1 and Our Thoughts on the Business
summary: SpaceX's IPO prospectus has been released, revealing key details about the company's financials and business model. Analysts share their thoughts on the implications for the space industry.

title: Blue Origin’s New Glenn Explodes on the Pad
summary: Blue Origin's New Glenn rocket suffered a catastrophic failure during its launch attempt, resulting in an explosion on the launch pad. The incident marks a significant setback for the company's orbital launch ambitions.

title: Europe Celebrates its Sovereignty Progress at SmallSat
summary: Europe showcased progress in achieving orbital sovereignty at SmallSat Europe. The event highlighted concrete advancements in the region's space capabilities.

title: Observable Space Raises $90M Series A
summary: Observable Space has raised $90 million in a Series A funding round to expand its production of laser and optical hardware and grow internationally beyond the US.

title: Space Markets Emerges From Stealth With Coinbase Ventures Investment
summary: Space Markets has emerged from stealth mode with a new investment from Coinbase Ventures. The company aims to create futures markets for space-related commodities and resources.

title: Exclusive: Stellar Alpina Raises $4.5M to Build Rotating Detonation Engines
summary: Stellar Alpina, a European propulsion startup, has raised $4.5 million to develop rotating detonation engines, a novel technology aimed at advancing spacecraft propulsion. This funding will support the company's efforts to bring this innovative thrust method to the commercial space market.

title: Aitech Upgrades its Space Supercomputer
summary: Aitech has upgraded its space supercomputer by integrating NVIDIA's IGX Thor platform into the S-A2300 COTS AI Supercomputer, significantly enhancing in-orbit data processing capabilities for customers.

title: NASA Awards LTV, Lunar Transport Contracts
summary: NASA has awarded contracts for the Lunar Terrain Vehicle (LTV) and lunar transport services, marking a significant step toward returning humans to the Moon. NASA Administrator Jared Isaacman hinted at an imminent 'grand return' during a press conference.

title: Mexican Airline Viva Taps SES to Connect 100 Aircraft
summary: Mexican low-cost airline Viva has partnered with SES to provide multi-orbit connectivity to 100 Airbus aircraft. SES will equip 60 Airbus A320s and 40 Airbus A321s with satellite connectivity services.

title: Northrop Grumman to Team With Apex for Space-Based Interceptors, Targeting Capability in 2027
summary: Northrop Grumman is partnering with space startup Apex to develop space-based interceptors (SBIs) with an on-orbit capability targeted for 2027. The collaboration aims to enhance space defense capabilities.

title: Viasat to Provide Satcom for Lockheed Martin-built ‘Hurricane Hunter’ Aircraft
summary: Viasat will supply a hybrid satcom solution for two Lockheed Martin-built aircraft in NOAA’s 'Hurricane Hunter' program. The specialized planes are used for direct hurricane monitoring and data collection.

title: Polish Space Agency Exec Outlines Poland’s Growing Space Ambitions
summary: The Polish Space Agency, founded in 2014, has significantly boosted Poland's commercial space sector, making it one of the top seven contributors in the field.

title: PLD Space to Invest Millions in French Guiana Launch Site
summary: PLD Space is investing millions to develop a launch site in French Guiana, one of the world's most significant spaceports. The company aims to expand its European launch capabilities with this strategic move.

title: SpaceX Gets Nearly $4.2 Billion Nod From Space Force For Initial SB-AMTI
summary: SpaceX received a nearly $4.2 billion contract from the U.S. Space Force for the initial Space Based Advanced Missile Threat Identification (SB-AMTI) program. This follows a $2.3 billion award for the Space Data Network Backbone, a LEO communications constellation.

title: Sateliot Wins New Telenor IoT Deal
summary: Sateliot, a Spanish satellite company, has secured a new IoT partnership with Telenor IoT, a division of the Nordic telco giant Telenor Group.

title: Viasat Full Year ‘26 Takeaways: Record Revenue, Equatys Moves Forward
summary: Viasat reported record revenue and awards for the full year 2026, though it did not meet its projected stabilization in maritime and fixed broadband. The company also highlighted progress on its Equatys initiative.

title: Blue Origin’s Launch Pad Explosion Could Impact Timelines for Commercial Customers, NASA Missions
summary: Blue Origin's New Glenn rocket suffered a launch pad explosion at Cape Canaveral, potentially delaying commercial and NASA missions. The incident marks a significant setback for the company's spaceflight timelines.

title: EU Targets User Terminal Tech Advancements Ahead of IRIS²
summary: The European Union has issued a tender for innovative and cost-effective user terminal technologies for the IRIS² satellite constellation. This move aims to advance user terminal tech ahead of the constellation's deployment.
